commit.message:
feat: cascade-delete domain and app children on deletion

When deleting a domain, all 18 child tables are cleaned up in
dependency order. App deletion cascades through its domains.
Prevents orphaned records without requiring SQLite CASCADE.

Also adds migration status fix documentation for Abdi.

commit.message:
feat(filter): version info in search, backfill fix, per-command deprecation (#272)

## Summary

- **Search version info**: Add `introduced_at`/`deprecated_at` fields to
server search response (`FilterSummary`, `FilterDetails`) and CLI client
types. `tokf search` now shows a VERSION column in table mode and
version/deprecated badges in interactive mode.
- **TypeScript barrel exports**: Add 4 missing re-exports
(`FilterVersionInfo`, `VersionedCatalogEntry`, `CommandGroup`,
`GroupedCatalog`) to `catalog.ts`.
- **Backfill fix**: `git ls-tree` returns exit 0 with empty output for
non-existent paths, so the `or_else` fallback in `list_filters_at_tag`
never fired for pre-workspace tags. Changed to `is_empty()` check so
filters from pre-0.2.3 tags are properly discovered.
- **Per-command deprecation**: Add `deprecate_same_command()` that runs
for each newly published filter, deprecating other stdlib filters with
the same `command_pattern`. This fixes single-filter CI publishes never
triggering deprecation.
- **Deduplicate semver guard**: Extract `is_version_current()` helper
used by both `run_deprecation_sweep` and `deprecate_same_command`.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test --workspace` — 1749 passed, 0 failed
- [x] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean
- [x] `cargo fmt -- --check` — clean
- [ ] Manual: `cargo run -p tokf -- backfill-versions --dry-run` shows
`introduced_at` for pre-0.2.3 filters
- [ ] Verify TypeScript imports resolve in tokf-net

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix barrier draw order, stars in PHOTO L3, debug cleanup, spawn cutoff

- Barrier depthTest: removed depthTest:false so barriers render in
  correct Z order instead of drawing on top of closer objects
- Stars enabled in PHOTO L3+ (S2 L3+) in both spawn logic and intro
- Debug cleanup: removed dbg-strip div, dbg-gs overlay, SECTOR DEBUG
  console.log, document.title state logging, hamburger debug logs,
  WebGL context error logging. _dbg is now a no-op.
- Spawn cutoff tightened from 76% to 72% of sector distance to prevent
  objects (especially barrier+ramp combos) from appearing past the
  finish ring which spawns at 80%
- Content objects in PHOTO: spawn logic confirmed working — content
  only spawns for uncollected items in that zone

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: translate GREATEST/LEAST to SQLite scalar MAX/MIN (#20)

GREATEST(v1, v2, ...) → MAX(v1, v2, ...)
LEAST(v1, v2, ...)    → MIN(v1, v2, ...)

SQLite's MAX/MIN are scalar (not aggregate) when called with 2+ arguments,
giving identical semantics to Snowflake's GREATEST/LEAST including NULL
propagation. Implemented as simple name-swap rules in the SIMPLE_RULES
vector. Eight integration tests and two unit tests added.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs(data-modeling): add per-calculation detection levels across 6 documents (#22) (#22)

Detection levels are per-calculation, not per-model. A calculation like
large_trading_activity can run at multiple grains (product, product×account).
Adds calc_detection_levels junction table, detection_level_universe
materialized table, detection_level_id in calc_results, separation of
computation from scoring, and Appendix E worked example.

commit.message:
Fix AI badge display: change ai_categorization column from text to jsonb

The ai_categorization column was stored as text, but the frontend expects
a parsed JSON object for badge rendering. PostgREST returns text columns
as strings, so properties like .category, .is_urgent were always undefined.
Changed to jsonb so PostgREST returns parsed objects. Updated TS types.

  columns to review_request table with migration

  (if review_requests feature enabled)
- Processor: hourly setInterval processes pending requests past the
  configured delay, sends SMS/email, and sends one follow-up after
  5 days with no click
- Tracking: GET /api/reviews/track/:id/click marks clickedAt and
  redirects to the tenant's Google review URL
- Settings: reviewRequestEnabled, reviewRequestDelay, reviewChannel,
  reviewSmsTemplate, googleReviewUrl stored in company.settings
- Routes: GET /api/reviews, GET /api/reviews/stats,
  GET/PUT /api/reviews/settings
- Frontend: Reviews page with Dashboard tab (stats cards + requests
  table) and Settings tab (enable toggle, delay, channel, template
  editor, Google URL input). Feature-gated behind review_requests
  (already in pro/business/construction tiers)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix large-bar overlap: zone clipping, glow ring bounds, controls layout

- Add per-zone QPainter setClipRect() in paintEvent() — prevents any
  zone's content from bleeding into adjacent zones at large sizes
- Fix glow ring overflow: at size3 (h=128), ring_r reached 62px while
  dx was only rz.x()+31, painting 31px off the left widget edge;
  clamp dx to ensure rings stay within widget bounds
- Fix controls zone overlap: bw_ was unbounded (h//2 = 64 at size3),
  causing gear/close buttons to encroach on OBS label at 1920px;
  cap bw_ at zone_width//4 and compute label_right as fixed margin
  from button area so OBS label always has clear space
- At size3, disk warning now stacks vertically below OBS label

commit.message:
fix: guest browsing redirected to login — remove aggressive 401 handler and guard auth hooks

The handle401 redirect added in the previous audit (W1) fired on 401 responses
from authenticated hooks on guest-browsable pages, breaking the browse-first
experience. Removed handle401 and added enabled: !!user guards to auth hooks
on /subscriptions, /appointments/book, and /home-services so they don't fire
for unauthenticated guests.

commit.message:
fix(mobile): verify BUG-001 mic release via removePublishedTrack — field tested

Three fix iterations for Android mic release on mute:
1. setAndroidAudioConfiguration(media) — insufficient (AudioRecord still held)
2. ADM stopLocalRecording() — insufficient (PeerConnection overrides)
3. removePublishedTrack() — works (removes RtpSender, triggers SDP renegotiation)

Remove unused dart:io and flutter_webrtc imports. Close task 005.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix boss bugs: bubble glitch, spawn distance, capture timer, SFX, score flash

- Remove ctx.clip() from bell body layers 3/4/5 — fixes phantom bubble circles
- Boss spawn distance: Math.max(1900, ...) so boss never starts near BH
- Boss capture timer: >=180 with one-time flag guard (no frame-skip miss)
- Boss hit SFX: replace bad sawtooth/square with punchy two-tone thwack
- Boss hit: show -500 score flash on boss (damage indicator, not +points)

feat: テストカバレッジ拡充 + is_branch_merged ロジック修正

テスト 13 → 20 に増加:
- capture_dirty_diff: clean/tracked/untracked の3ケース
- is_branch_merged: マージ済み=true / 未分岐=false の2ケース
- load_config: invalid KDL（未閉じ文字列・構文エラー）の2ケース

is_branch_merged の false-positive ガードを修正:
- 旧: rev-list origin/main..HEAD --count == 0（マージ後も0を返すバグ）
- 新: git rev-parse で HEAD と origin/main の SHA を直接比較

fix: remove overly broad "killed process" pattern from oom detection (#4127)

* fix: remove overly broad "killed process" pattern from oom detection

The "killed process" string matches any kernel process kill, not just
OOM kills. The remaining patterns (out of memory, oom-kill, oom_reaper)
already cover all OOM scenarios precisely.

Closes #4126
